source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/240515 ASPEN IODR FDA 대응/Abyz-FDA-20240515/#Abyz/14. Risk Management/11.Appendix1. Risk Management FMEA attached.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk Management FMEA content for AspenView software hazards, risk controls, residual risk, and risk/benefit references under EN ISO 14971:2012.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports risk management documentation by identifying hazards, estimating risk, documenting controls, verifying implementation, and evaluating residual risk and benefit.
keyPoints: Hazard IDs R01 through R08 are listed across Exam Mode, Viewer Mode, Browser Mode, Setting Module, and Database Module.
Risk control categories are defined as D: inherent safety by design, P: protective measures, and I: information for safety.
Several hazards initially rated unacceptable are reduced to acceptable after controls.
Controls include alarm messages, user manual information, training/manual service, code review, dynamic test, and software validation references.
Residual risk entries refer to risk/benefit analysis tables in the Risk management report.

entities.name: Image Processing Tool.exe
entities.evidence: Designing to show the set pixel value in "Image Processing Tool.exe"

concepts.name: Protective measures
concepts.evidence: P: Protective measures in the medical device itself or in themanufacturing process

evidence.claim: The document references EN ISO 14971:2012 risk management sections.
The FMEA table includes hazard identification, risk estimation, risk control, residual risk, and risk/benefit analysis fields.
evidence.evidenceText: 4.3 Identification of hazards 4.4 Estimation of the risk(s) for each hazardous situation 6.2 Risk control option analysis 6.3 Implementation of risk control measure(s) 6.4 Residual risk evaluation 6.5 Risk/benefit analysis

evidence.claim: Risk control types are categorized as design, protective measures, and information for safety.
evidence.evidenceText: D: Inherent safety by design P: Protective measures in the medical device itself or in themanufacturing process I: Information for safety

evidence.claim: Exam Mode R01 addresses mismatched pixel data as an operational use-error hazard.
evidence.evidenceText: Exam Mode R01 Operational hazard (Use error) Mismatched Pixel data

evidence.claim: Exam Mode R02 addresses wrong detector setting information that may lead to unintended excessive X-ray exposure.
evidence.evidenceText: Exam Mode R02 Operational hazard (Use error) Input wrong information(Detector setting) Second X-ray photographing is tried due to a failure of the first one Unintended excessive X ray is applied

evidence.claim: Exam Mode R03 addresses acquiring an unknowable image without selecting the patient or study.
evidence.evidenceText: Exam Mode R03 Information hazard Acqurie the unknowable image whitout selecting the patient/study

evidence.claim: Viewer Mode R04 addresses export failures caused by missing USB or invalid HDD path.
evidence.evidenceText: Viewer Mode R04 Information hazard No insert the USB or invalid HDD Path(To export the images)

evidence.claim: Browser Mode R05 addresses failure to select a patient or study before deleting images.
evidence.evidenceText: Browser Mode R05 Information hazard No select the patient/studyto delete images

evidence.claim: Setting Module R06 addresses wrong MWL information and limited usage of the MWL function.
evidence.evidenceText: Setting Module R06 Information hazard Wrong information (MWL) Limited usage(Use MWL function) cuased by wrong input information

evidence.claim: Database Module R07 addresses failure to verify patient and studies, causing mixed patient information.
evidence.evidenceText: Database Module R07 Operational hazard (Use error) Failed to verify the patient and Studies Took the unintended images Mixed the patient information

evidence.claim: Database Module R08 addresses database damage caused by PC power turning off by physician mistake.
evidence.evidenceText: Database Module R08 Operational hazard (Use error) PC power truns off byphysician's mistake Damage the database Mixed the data

evidence.claim: Alarm messages are used as risk controls to inform the user.
evidence.evidenceText: Alram message are used to inform the user.

evidence.claim: The user manual RS-AspenView-UM is used for information-for-safety controls.
evidence.evidenceText: Serve the training and Manual User Manual (RS-AspenView-UM)

evidence.claim: Software validation test references are used to verify risk control measures.
Several residual risk entries state no new or increased risks.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in the source text; date-like strings appear only in the source path.
Revision or version is not present in the source text.
FDA appears as authoritySignal and in the source path, but the document body does not identify FDA as an issuing or reviewing authority.
OCR/text quality is low, with misspellings and spacing errors such as 'Riskevaluation', 'Alram', 'truns', and merged words.
